data.description: The latest figures show that the global economy is projected to grow by 6% in 2021, with the United States leading the recovery with an expected growth rate of 6.4%. The European Union is expected to see growth of 4.2%, while China's economy is predicted to expand by 8.4%. In terms of job losses, the global economy experienced a decline of 8.8% in working hours in 2020, equivalent to 255 million full-time jobs. This has had a significant impact on global poverty rates, with an estimated 119-124 million people falling into extreme poverty last year. In order to combat this trend, global vaccination efforts are crucial to jumpstart economic recovery and reduce poverty levels worldwide.
